description: Lift s3-tests headline pass rate from 80.1% (141/176) to ≥94% by closing the 26 real S3 surface gaps clustered into multipart per-part API (per-part offset tracking + ?partNumber=N GET + composite checksum response shape + multipart copy FlexibleChecksum), versioning literal 'null' version-id semantics (PUT to unversioned bucket later enabled, plus Suspended-versioning), listing edge cases (delimiter+prefix, V2 opaque continuation token), multipart preconditions (If-Match / If-None-Match on Complete), size-too-small + resend-ordering edge cases, and CompleteMultipartUpload SHA256 input validation. 8 SigV2 fails are deliberate gaps. Non-UTF-8 prefix + V1 marker semantics + allow-unordered + encoding-type edge cases are explicitly out of scope. Modern boto / aws-cli 2.x / Athena / Glue / Snowflake all exercise the kept paths. Closing all 26 in-scope failures lifts headline to 167/176 = 94.9%; the floor target is ≥90%.

userStories.title: Multipart per-part offset tracking in data.Manifest
userStories.description: As a developer fixing ?partNumber=N GET, I need each part's byte-range to be queryable from the manifest so a ranged GET can serve the exact bytes of part N without scanning the whole object.
acceptanceCriteria: Add PartChunks []PartRange field to data.Manifest. Struct: PartNumber int, Offset int64, Size int64, ETag string, ChecksumValue string, ChecksumAlgorithm string
Field is optional (json:',omitempty' + matching protobuf field number in manifest.proto + helper updates in manifest_codec.go)

Protobuf field-number reservation: manifest.proto already uses 1-6 + 12 (BackendRef + SSE from prior cycle), with 7-11 reserved. PartChunks gets field number 13 — first available after the existing reservation block. Update the field-number assignment header comment at the top of manifest.proto to document US-s3-tests-90 reserving 13 for PartChunks (and 14..18 for the PartRange sub-message fields if ever flattened)
CompleteMultipartUpload populates PartChunks from the multipart_uploads.parts rows on the LWT-success path
Single-PUT objects leave PartChunks nil (not an empty slice — nil sentinels 'not multipart')
Both encodings (JSON + protobuf) round-trip via existing data.EncodeManifest / DecodeManifest
Migration: existing rows decode with PartChunks=nil and serve normally (backward-compatible)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: PartChunks populated by meta layer (memory + cassandra) at CompleteMultipartUpload LWT-success time, capturing PartNumber/Offset/Size/ETag for each part. Checksum fields land empty in this story; US-004 will write them at UploadPart time via a new MultipartPart.ChecksumValue/Algorithm pair. Backend pass-through (US-010) path also gets PartChunks — both shape variants now carry per-part offsets so US-002 ?partNumber=N GET works regardless of which data backend served the parts. Contract test caseCompleteMultipartPopulatesPartChunks runs against memory and cassandra (under -tags integration) lock-step.

userStories.title: ?partNumber=N GET serves the exact part body
userStories.description: As an S3 client, when I GET an object with ?partNumber=3, I expect to receive only the bytes of part 3, plus headers x-amz-mp-parts-count and Content-Range: bytes <off>-<end>/<total>.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/s3api/server.go::getObject (line ~493) handles ?partNumber=N query param when uploadId is NOT set: looks up PartChunks[N-1] from the decoded manifest. The existing ?partNumber=N + uploadId path in server.go around line 393 (UploadPart for in-progress multipart) is unchanged
Returns 416 InvalidRange when N <= 0 or N > len(PartChunks)
Returns 416 for non-multipart objects when partNumber is set
Sets response headers: x-amz-mp-parts-count, Content-Range, Content-Length=<part size>, ETag of the part (not whole object)
Streams [Offset, Offset+Size) of the underlying chunk stream
Range: bytes=... combined with ?partNumber= is part-relative (offsets resolve within the part, not the whole object)
s3-tests test_multipart_*_get_part (3 tests) pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Wired internal/s3api/server.go::getObject to dispatch ?partNumber=N (without uploadId) to a new getObjectPart helper. PartChunks[N-1] gives Offset/Size/ETag; out-of-range or non-multipart returns 416 InvalidPartNumber. Range header stacks part-relative — absolute byte range is shifted by pr.Offset, Content-Range carries absolute coords against whole-object total (matches AWS observable). Status is always 206 Partial Content for partNumber GETs. ETag returned is the part ETag (not whole-object). HEAD path mirrors the GET path (same headers, no body). New ErrInvalidPartNumber added to errors.go (HTTP 416). Test coverage in internal/s3api/multipart_partnumber_test.go: 3-part MPU, asserts part-2 body, HEAD part-3 headers, part-relative Range, part ETag != whole, and three 416 cases (N=4, N=0, N=-1, single-PUT object).

userStories.title: Per-part composite-checksum response shape
userStories.description: As an S3 client using SDK auto-checksum, the multipart CompleteMultipartUpload response must carry the composite checksum AND ?partNumber=N HEAD/GET must echo per-part checksums in x-amz-checksum-<algo> headers when ChecksumMode=ENABLED.
acceptanceCriteria: CompleteMultipartUploadResult XML body includes <ChecksumCRC32>, <ChecksumCRC32C>, <ChecksumSHA1>, <ChecksumSHA256> (whichever was set on Initiate) — composite computed across part checksums per AWS formula (concatenate raw part-digests, SHA-256 of concat, base64)
Response also includes <ChecksumType>FULL_OBJECT</ChecksumType> or COMPOSITE matching what was set on Initiate
HEAD object with x-amz-checksum-mode: ENABLED returns the composite checksum header
HEAD object with ?partNumber=N + ChecksumMode=ENABLED returns the per-part checksum from PartChunks[N-1].ChecksumValue
s3-tests test_multipart_use_cksum_helper_* (5 tests) pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Initiate reads x-amz-checksum-algorithm + x-amz-checksum-type (default COMPOSITE) and stamps them on meta.MultipartUpload. UploadPart captures the client's per-part x-amz-checksum-<algo> header onto meta.MultipartPart and echoes it on the response. Complete computes the COMPOSITE hash-of-hashes per AWS formula (base64(HASH(d1||d2||...))-N) using the named algorithm, OR adopts the client-supplied header value for FULL_OBJECT type. Composite + algo + type land on data.Manifest.MultipartChecksum* fields and surface in CompleteMultipartUploadResult XML and on HEAD/GET when x-amz-checksum-mode=ENABLED. Per-part HEAD/GET (?partNumber=N) emits PartChunks[N-1].ChecksumValue. Cassandra ALTERs add checksum_algorithm + checksum_type to multipart_uploads, checksum_value + checksum_algorithm to multipart_parts. Meta CompleteMultipartUpload preAssembled-sentinel tightened from `obj.Manifest != nil` to `obj.Manifest != nil && obj.Manifest.BackendRef != nil` so a hint-only manifest (composite-checksum carrier) does not short-circuit chunks-shape assembly. Test coverage in internal/s3api/multipart_checksum_test.go: COMPOSITE round-trip across SHA256/SHA1/CRC32/CRC32C, FULL_OBJECT pass-through (CRC32), and opt-out negative case.

userStories.title: Multipart copy FlexibleChecksum path
userStories.description: As an S3 client copying a multipart object via UploadPartCopy, the boto SDK auto-generates a checksum on the copy body and expects the gateway to recompute and validate it. Today we accept the body without checksum validation, which the SDK's FlexibleChecksumError catches.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/s3api/multipart.go::uploadPartCopy (line ~148) reads x-amz-checksum-algorithm header from request
When set, recompute the named checksum over the body bytes during streaming (do not buffer)
Compare with x-amz-checksum-<algo> header value if client supplied; mismatch returns BadDigest
Store the computed checksum in multipart_uploads.parts[N].checksum_value so US-001's PartChunks carries it forward
s3-tests test_multipart_copy_* (4 tests) pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Listing — delimiter+prefix interaction
userStories.description: As an S3 client listing with both prefix and delimiter, the response must group keys under the prefix into CommonPrefixes based on the delimiter, with NextMarker reflecting the last grouped prefix correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/s3api/server.go::listObjects (line ~272) handles prefix=foo/&delimiter=/ by extracting the substring between the prefix end and the next delimiter occurrence in each key
CommonPrefixes deduplicates within a page, in lexical order
NextMarker (V1) / NextContinuationToken (V2) is set to the last key OR the last CommonPrefix returned, whichever is lexically later
s3-tests test_bucket_list_delimiter_prefix* pass (currently 1-2 failures in this category)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Listing — V2 continuation token as opaque base64
userStories.description: As an S3 client paging through ListObjectsV2, the ContinuationToken is an opaque token (AWS-style: base64 of an internal struct). Today we treat it as a literal marker, which fails when the marker contains base64-decode-incompatible bytes.
acceptanceCriteria: ContinuationToken in response is base64 of a struct {Marker string; Shard int} (JSON-encoded then base64)
?continuation-token=<token> request parses the token back into Marker + Shard
Backward compat: tokens that don't decode as base64-JSON fall back to the literal-marker interpretation (so existing clients in flight don't break mid-page)
V1 marker parameter unchanged — opaque tokens are V2-only
s3-tests test_bucket_listv2_continuationtoken* pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Versioning — literal 'null' version-id semantics
userStories.description: As an S3 client, when I PUT an object into an unversioned bucket and later enable versioning, the original object must be addressable via versionId=null. PUTs while versioning is suspended also produce null-versioned objects.
acceptanceCriteria: meta.Object.VersionID accepts the literal string 'null' for unversioned-bucket PUTs and suspended-bucket PUTs
?versionId=null on GET / HEAD / DELETE resolves the row with VersionID='null'
On versioning Enable + subsequent overwrite, the prior null version is preserved (not deleted; new version generated UUID)
On versioning Suspend + new PUT, any existing null version is overwritten (DELETE old null + INSERT new null) atomically via LWT
ListObjectVersions shows null rows in clustering order alongside UUID versions
Implementation lockstep across all three meta backends: memory, cassandra (LWT-on-LWT for the suspend-replace invariant), tikv (pessimistic txn for the same invariant)
s3-tests test_versioning_obj_plain_null_version_* + test_versioning_obj_suspend* (5 tests) pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Multipart — Complete preconditions (If-Match / If-None-Match)
userStories.description: As an S3 client completing a multipart upload conditionally, the gateway must gate the LWT path on If-Match / If-None-Match headers referring to the eventual object ETag (not the upload ID).
acceptanceCriteria: internal/s3api/multipart.go::completeMultipart (line ~239) reads If-Match / If-None-Match from the request
When If-None-Match: * is set and the object exists, returns 412 PreconditionFailed
When If-Match is set and does NOT match the existing object's ETag, returns 412 PreconditionFailed
Precondition check happens BEFORE the LWT flip (so a conflicting Complete attempt does not leak 'completing' state)
VersionId header is set on the response when the bucket is versioned
s3-tests test_multipart_put_object_if_* + test_multipart_put_current_object_if_match pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Multipart — size-too-small + resend-ordering edge cases
userStories.description: As an S3 client, the gateway must reject CompleteMultipartUpload when any non-last part is smaller than 5 MiB, AND must handle the case where a resent part finishes after a later part (resend-first-finishes-last).
acceptanceCriteria: internal/s3api/multipart.go::completeMultipart enumerates parts in order, asserts every part except the last is ≥5 MiB (5*1024*1024 bytes); returns EntityTooSmall on violation
When two UploadPart calls land for the same part number with different ETags (race), the LATER write wins (LWT on multipart_uploads.parts keyed on part_number; same shape on memory + tikv)
CompleteMultipartUpload body's <Part> list specifies ETags; mismatch with stored part ETag = InvalidPart
s3-tests test_multipart_upload_size_too_small, test_multipart_upload_resend_part, test_multipart_resend_first_finishes_last pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Multipart — CompleteMultipartUpload SHA256 input validation
userStories.description: As an S3 client supplying a composite-checksum SHA256 on CompleteMultipartUpload, the gateway must validate the supplied digest against the recomputed value and reject mismatches.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/s3api/multipart.go::completeMultipart reads x-amz-checksum-sha256 from request headers
When set, recomputes the composite SHA-256 over part digests and compares; mismatch returns BadDigest
Same handling for CRC32, CRC32C, SHA1
s3-tests test_multipart_checksum_sha256 passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Refresh s3-tests baseline + ROADMAP close-flip
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the latest s3-tests run captured in scripts/s3-tests/README.md and the ROADMAP P1 flipped to Done.
acceptanceCriteria: Run scripts/s3-tests/run.sh against a clean make up-all stack
Append a new section to scripts/s3-tests/README.md baseline list: ### YYYY-MM-DD — <commit-sha> with the pass-rate line + remaining failure breakdown
Verify pass rate ≥90% (target ≥94%; closing all 26 in-scope failures reaches 94.9%)
If <90%: file the remaining gaps as new P1/P2 entries under appropriate ROADMAP sections; do NOT flip the headline
If ≥90%: flip ROADMAP P1 entry to Done close-flip format with the closing SHA (or `(commit pending)` per CLAUDE.md follow-up SHA edit rule)

Document test_bucket_list_prefix_unreadable, test_bucket_list_unordered, test_bucket_create_exists, and any V1 ListObjects / encoding-type / SDK-quirk failures as deliberate gaps in scripts/s3-tests/README.md (not as new ROADMAP entries) so they stop reading as open work
Update headline number in ROADMAP.md introduction: **80.1% (141/176)** → **<new>% (<n>/176)**
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
